region_of_origin: United Kingdom (brand origin); sold globally including Singapore

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301638 (carbonated water) is the closest parent; no brand-specific Schweppes soda water entry exists in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_description: Water (including mineral water and carbonated water)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN000A covers water including carbonated water; no brand-specific code exists for Schweppes soda water.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 11713004 (Water) is the closest defensible parent concept; carbonated water is not separately coded as a distinct SNOMED CT food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 320ml can of Schweppes Soda Water, as typically served in Singapore and food delivery contexts.

nutrient_source.title: Carbonated water, plain — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 14429)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Schweppes Soda Water is carbonated water with no added sugars, flavours, or sodium beyond trace mineral content. USDA FoodData Central entry for plain carbonated water (seltzer/club soda) is an exact compositional match. Values confirmed consistent with Schweppes product label data (0 kcal, 0g macros, ~0mg sodium per 100ml).

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of beverages — water and carbonated water convention
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain carbonated water contains no glycemic carbohydrates (0g carbohydrate per 100g). The University of Sydney GI Database does not assign a formal GI to water or plain soda water as there is no glycemic response. By established convention, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value) to indicate effectively zero glycemic impact. gi_category is 'low'. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate_g is 0.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium content (~5mg per 100ml / ~16mg per 320ml can)
hypertension.guidance: Schweppes Soda Water contains only trace sodium (~16mg per 320ml can), well below the threshold of concern for hypertensive individuals, and can be consumed freely as a hydrating beverage without meaningful impact on blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrates and zero sugar; no glycemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: Schweppes Soda Water contains no sugar or carbohydrates and has no glycemic impact, making it a safe and suitable hydration choice for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes without any need for carbohydrate counting.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero cholesterol, zero sugar; neutral cardiovascular profile
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Schweppes Soda Water contains no fat, cholesterol, or sugar, and its negligible sodium content poses no meaningful cardiovascular risk, making it a heart-healthy beverage choice for individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Schweppes Soda Water (320ml Can) is a commercially produced plain carbonated water beverage manufactured by the Schweppes brand, a globally recognised soft drink company with origins in the United Kingdom. The product consists of water infused with carbon dioxide under pressure to produce effervescence, with no added sugars, flavourings, sweeteners, or significant mineral content beyond trace sodium. It is packaged in a 320ml aluminium can and is widely available in Singapore, cafes, and food delivery platforms. The beverage is completely calorie-free, containing zero energy, zero protein, zero fat, and zero carbohydrates per 100ml, making it nutritionally equivalent to plain sparkling water. It has no glycemic impact and is suitable for all dietary patterns including vegetarian, vegan, halal, kosher, and gluten-free diets. Clinically, it is appropriate for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease, as it contributes no sugar, fat, or meaningful sodium load. The product is commonly served as a mixer for alcoholic beverages, as a palate cleanser, or as a refreshing non-caloric alternative to sugary soft drinks. In Singapore food culture, soda water is frequently ordered alongside Chinese cuisine meals or used as a base for mocktails and cocktails.
common_user_queries: Is Schweppes Soda Water calorie free?
Is Schweppes Soda Water good for hypertension?
What is the difference between soda water and sparkling water?
How many calories in a can of soda water?

confidence_reason: Schweppes Soda Water is a well-characterised commercial product consisting solely of carbonated water. Nutritional values (all zero macros, trace sodium) are confirmed by USDA FoodData Central data for plain carbonated water and are consistent with Schweppes product label information. No estimation or analogy was required for macronutrients.
notes: Zero-macro justification: energy_kcal is naturally zero as this is plain carbonated water with no caloric content. protein_g is naturally zero — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; soda water contains no protein by formulation. fat_g is naturally zero — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; soda water contains no fat by formulation. carbohydrate_g is naturally zero — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; soda water contains no carbohydrates by formulation. sugar_g is naturally zero by product formulation. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as this is a liquid beverage with no fibre. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are all naturally zero by formulation. cholesterol_mg is naturally zero. Sodium_mg is approximately 5mg per 100ml (~16mg per 320ml can) based on USDA data for plain carbonated water; this is a trace amount. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reliably reported in the USDA source for plain carbonated water (varies by brand mineral content). phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for plain carbonated water. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for plain carbonated water. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for plain carbonated water. Vitamin values (vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1 through b12, folate, vitamin_d, vitamin_e) are confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central — naturally zero as this is plain water with no vitamin content. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate_g is 0. Serving size of 320ml (320g) reflects the standard can size as listed on field survey data. Price SGD 5.20 preserved from input (field survey data listing, See Ya Tonight restaurant). Cuisine tagged as Global/International as Schweppes is a global brand; input cuisine tag 'Chinese' reflects the restaurant context rather than the product origin.

improvement_actions_taken: Applied zero-macro justification rule: documented 'naturally zero' and 'confirmed zero' phrases in notes for all zero core macros.
Applied zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule: set glycemic_index=1 (sentinel), gi_category='low', glycemic_load_per_serving=0.0.
Applied sentinel values documentation rule: documented all -1 values (calcium_mg, iron_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, selenium_mcg) in notes with source explanation.
Applied ontology match_type rule: used only 'exact', 'closest_parent', or 'none' values for all three ontology match_type fields.
Applied aliases consistency rule: verified all aliases_by_language entries appear verbatim in flat aliases list.
Applied multilingual naming rule for Singaporean context: populated Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names and aliases.
Corrected food_name to canonical English form removing Chinese characters from the primary name field.
Set cuisine to Global/International with note explaining input Chinese tag reflects restaurant context.
